title: amphilophium lactiflorum
intro: Amphilophium lactiflorum, also known as the white-flowered amphilophium, is a flowering plant species in the Bignonaceae family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and fever.
